title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXVII.
content:
Would that I were a tuneful lyre,
        Of burnished ivory fair,
    Which, in the Dionysian choir,
        Some blooming boy should bear!

    Would that I were a golden vase.
        That some bright nymph might hold
    My spotless frame, with blushing grace,
        Herself as pure as gold!
meaning:
Thomas Moore's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Odes Of Anacreon - Ode LXXVII."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
